title: Formulations and delivery methods for reduction and elimination of pathogen induced inflammations in the human body
abstract: A formulation consisting of at least one Alpha Hydroxy Acid in a carrier composition useful for the treatment of inflammation arising from the presence of microbiofilms on tissues of the human body where pathogens nucleate and reproduce. In one aspect, the formulation results in a breakdown, and at least partial elimination of, the microbiofilm.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A formulation consisting of at least one Alpha Hydroxy Acid in a carrier composition useful for the treatment of inflammation arising from the presence of microbiofilms on tissues of the human body where pathogens nucleate and reproduce. 2. The formulation of claim 1 where the Alpha Hydroxyde Acid is 2- Hydroxypropanoic acid, 2-Hydroxypropane-1 ,2,3-tricarboxylic acid, or 2- Hydroxypropane-1 ,2,3-tricarboxylic acid. 3. A formulation consisting of at least two Alpha Hydroxy Acids in a carrier composition useful for the treatment of inflammation arising from the presence of microbiofilm on tissues of the human body where pathogens nucleate and reproduce. 4. The formulation of claim 3 where the at least two Alpha Hydroxyde Acids consist of a combination of 2-Hydroxypropanoic acid and 2-Hydroxypropane- 1 ,2,3-tricarboxylic acid. 5. The formulation of claims 2 and 4 where the concentration of 2- Hydroxypropanoic acid is between 0.25 and 15 percent, and preferable 5 to 10 percent. 6. The formulation of claims 2 and 4 where the concentration of 2- Hydroxypropane-1 ,2,3-tricarboxylic acid is between 0.2 and 7 percent, and preferable 1 to 5 percent. 7. The formulation of claim 2 and 4 where 2-Hydroxyethanoic acid is added with concentrations in the 0.01 to 0.2 percent and preferable in the 0.02 to 0.05 percent. 8. The formulation of claims 2 and 4 where calcium is added with a maximum concentration of 10%. 9. The formulation of claims 1 and 3 where the carrier composition is high purity sterile water or high purity sterile water with up to 10 percent glucose solution, and preferable in the 3 to 6 percent range. 10. The formulation of claims 1 and 3 where the carrier composition is a high viscosity high purity inert syrup based on high purity water containing high glucose concentration to yield a Brix between 70 and 90 and typically 85, with a density greater than 1.20g/ml, and typically 1.32 g/ml. 1 1. The formulation of claims 1 and 3 where the carrier composition is medium viscosity high purity inert syrup, based on high purity water containing medium glucose concentration to yield a Brix between 40 and 70 and typically 55. 12. The formulation of claims 1 and 3 where the carrier composition is a low viscosity high purity inert syrup, based on high purity water containing low glucose concentration to yield a Brix between 10 and 40 and typically 25. 13. The formulations of claims 9, 10, 1 1 and 12 where a flavor is added to facilitate the intake via the mouth. 14. The formulations of claims 9, 10, 1 1 and 12 where the AHAs molecules are encapsulated in an excipient in order to release the AHA based on either, body temperature, body chemicals, or both. 15. The formulation of claims 1 and 3 where the carrier composition is tooth paste. 16. The formulation of claims 1 and 3 where the carrier composition is mouth wash. 17. The formulation of claims 1 and 3 where the carrier is a biodegradable polymer coating a medical implant device such as an orthopedic or teeth implants, and/or a catheter, and/or any other implanted item. 18. A method for the delivery of formulation of claims 1 and 3, comprising spraying the composition into the nostrils twice daily for a period of a month. 19. A method for the delivery of formulation of claim 1 and 3, comprising spraying the composition into lymphatic tissue accessible through the mouth of the patient twice daily for a period of a month. 20. A method for delivery of the formulation of claims 1 and 3, comprising drinking one table spoon of the carrier composition syrup twice daily for a period of a month. 21. A method of delivery of the formulation of claims 1 and 3, comprising impregnating a hygienic paper towel with the carrier composition, sealing the hygienic paper towel in an air tight envelope until its use is needed, and then applying the carrier composition by hand to the vagina twice daily for a period of a month. 22. A method of delivery of the formulation of claims 1 and 3, comprising supplying the formulation through a catheter to introduce 1 to 100ml, preferable 5 to 20ml into the urinary system using manual compression pump at the external end of the catheter. 23. A method of claim 20 where the catheter is a three (3) way catheter wherein the formulation is introduced by the use of a soft mechanical pump or a manual compression pump, the solution is circulated into the affected area, and the solution is then cycled out via the out channel of the catheter. 24. A method of delivery of the formulation of claim 15, wherein a tooth brush is used to apply the tooth paste to the gums twice daily for a period of a month. 25. A method of delivery of the formulation of claim 16, wherein the mouth wash is used to deliver the formulation to the gums twice daily for a period of a month.
